ac_text: Mandatory fields name and primary phone must be provided.
title: Customer profile update succeeds when name and primary phone are both provided via UI
classification_rationale: Validates the happy-path business rule that mandatory fields name and primary phone allow a successful profile update through the UI.
preconditions: User account exists with role Customer_Service_Agent and has edit permission on Customer Profile module in OSS.
Customer record CUST-10045 exists in the system with current name 'Jane Doe' and primary phone '+1-555-0100'.
OSS application is deployed and accessible in UAT environment at https://oss-uat.telecom.local.
steps: Navigate to Customer Profile search page.
Enter 'CUST-10045' in the Customer ID search field.
Click the Search button.
Click the Edit button on the profile for CUST-10045.
Clear the Name field and type 'John Smith'.
Clear the Primary Phone field and type '+1-555-0199'.
Click the Save button.
expected_result: Profile update is saved successfully. A green confirmation banner displays 'Customer profile updated successfully.' The Name field shows 'John Smith' and Primary Phone field shows '+1-555-0199' on the profile detail page.
review_feedback: maintainability: Could be simplified

completeness.reason: Has preconditions, steps, test data, and expected result

defect_detection.reason: Primarily happy-path coverage

ac_text: Mandatory fields name and primary phone must be provided.

Customer record CUST-10045 exists in the database with name 'Jane Doe' and primary phone '+1-555-0100'.
steps: Send PUT request to /api/v1/customers/CUST-10045 with the specified request body and Authorization header.
Capture the HTTP response status code and response body.
expected_result: HTTP 200 OK is returned. Response body contains {"customerId": "CUST-10045", "name": "Maria Garcia", "primaryPhone": "+1-555-0250", "status": "updated"}. No error array is present in the response.

ac_text: Mandatory fields name and primary phone must be provided.
title: Profile update API response time remains under 2 seconds under concurrent load of 50 requests
classification_rationale: Non-functional performance test validating a single API feature's response time, not a cross-system flow, so classified as Functional.
preconditions: API endpoint PUT /api/v1/customers/{customerId} is deployed and reachable in performance test environment.
50 distinct customer records CUST-20001 through CUST-20050 exist in the database with valid name and primary phone values.
Load testing tool (e.g., JMeter) is configured with 50 concurrent threads targeting the endpoint.
Valid OAuth2 bearer tokens are pre-generated for each thread.
test_data: Request Body template: {"name": "PerfTest User N", "primaryPhone": "+1-555-2NNNN"} where N is the thread index
SLA threshold: 2000 ms per request (95th percentile)
steps: Configure the load testing tool with 50 concurrent threads each sending a PUT request to /api/v1/customers/{customerId} with unique customer IDs and valid mandatory fields.
Execute the load test for a duration of 60 seconds.
Collect response time metrics for all requests.
expected_result: 95th percentile response time is at or below 2000 ms. All 50 concurrent requests return HTTP 200. Error rate is 0%. No HTTP 5xx responses are observed.

ac_text: Mandatory fields name and primary phone must be provided.
title: Database correctly persists updated name and primary phone after successful profile update

expected_result: The API returns HTTP 200. The database query returns name = 'David S. Park' and primary_phone = '+1-555-0555'. The updated_at timestamp is within 5 seconds of the current UTC time.

ac_text: Unauthorized users cannot update customer profiles.
title: Security: Brute-force authorization bypass attempts are rate-limited and logged
classification_rationale: Validates a security control ensuring repeated unauthorized access attempts are throttled, directly supporting the authorization enforcement mandate of this AC.
preconditions: API endpoint PUT /api/v1/customers/{customerId} is deployed and accessible
Valid OAuth2 bearer token exists for user 'analyst.wong' with role Billing_Analyst (no edit permission)
Rate limiting is configured at 10 requests per minute per user for 4xx responses
Security audit log is enabled and accessible at /var/log/oss/security-audit.log
steps: Send 15 rapid sequential PUT requests to /api/v1/customers/CUST-10045 with the Billing_Analyst bearer token and JSON request body within 60 seconds.
Capture the HTTP response status code for each request.

expected_result: The first 10 requests each return HTTP 403 Forbidden. Requests 11 through 15 return HTTP 429 Too Many Requests with header 'Retry-After' present. The security audit log contains at least 15 entries for user 'analyst.wong' with action 'PROFILE_UPDATE_DENIED' and includes source IP address, timestamp, and customer ID 'CUST-10045' for each entry.

environment_readiness: Environment needs are under-specified

ac_text: Profile update should be reflected in downstream CRM sync jobs.
title: CRM sync API response time remains within SLA under expected concurrent profile update load
classification_rationale: This is a non-functional performance test scoped to the API sync trigger endpoint — categorized as Functional since it tests a single endpoint's performance characteristic.
preconditions: 100 distinct customer records (CUST-80001 through CUST-80100) exist in the OSS database, each with valid name, primary phone, and email
API endpoint PUT /api/v1/customers/{id} is deployed in UAT environment
CRM sync job infrastructure is active and can receive queue entries
Load testing tool (e.g., JMeter) is configured with 50 concurrent virtual users
SLA target: 95th percentile response time ≤ 2000ms for profile update API
steps: Configure JMeter test plan with 50 concurrent threads targeting PUT /api/v1/customers/{id} with randomized customer IDs from CUST-80001 to CUST-80100
Set ramp-up to 30 seconds and total duration to 5 minutes
Execute the load test
Collect response time percentiles (P50, P95, P99) and error rate from the test results
expected_result: P95 response time is ≤ 2000ms. P50 response time is ≤ 800ms. Error rate is < 1%. All successful responses return HTTP 200. No sync queue insertion failures are observed in the application logs during the test window.

ac_text: Profile update should be reflected in downstream CRM sync jobs.

classification_rationale: This test validates the ETL sync job's failure-handling logic when the downstream system is unavailable — a single-feature functional verification of error recovery.

CUST-60300 has sync_status 'PENDING' in crm_sync_queue with retry_count = 0

test_data: Expected HTTP error: Connection timeout or HTTP 503
steps: Trigger ETL sync job SYNC-JOB-3021 manually via the job scheduler console
Monitor the sync job execution log for CUST-60300
Query the crm_sync_queue table for CUST-60300 after job execution
Query the sync_error_log table for CUST-60300 error details
expected_result: ETL sync job logs an error entry in sync_error_log for CUST-60300 with error_type 'CONNECTION_FAILED' and the HTTP error detail (timeout or 503). crm_sync_queue record for CUST-60300 shows sync_status 'RETRY_PENDING', retry_count = 1, and next_retry_at is set to current time + 300 seconds. No data is written to downstream CRM. Job completes without crashing and continues processing other queued records.
